text.title: Growth Twitter X Growth Core Rules
text.summary: 5 testable rules every Growth Twitter X Growth application must enforce. These are HARD rules — user-supplied input cannot override them.

rule.statement: Positioning MUST be a unique-angle sentence ('I post X that nobody else does'); generic positioning is rejected.
rule.rationale: X compounds on perspective, not on generic content; without an angle the account plateaus.

rule.statement: Daily posting MUST be 3-5 standalone posts; <3 starves the algorithm and >5 burns the operator.
rule.rationale: X's algorithm needs a baseline post density to maintain reach; >5 risks burnout and content quality drop.

rule.statement: 1-2 long threads MUST ship per week; thread-free weeks lose audience depth.
rule.rationale: Threads compound profile-visit → follow rate by 3-5x; absence flattens growth.

rule.statement: Daily 20-30 replies MUST target accounts ≥5x operator's audience size; same-size replies don't surface.
rule.rationale: Reply visibility is dominated by the parent post's reach; replying to smaller accounts doesn't compound discovery.

rule.statement: KPI set MUST track replies_from_strangers + qualified_follows, not raw follower count.
rule.rationale: Follower count moves with bot-attracting posts; replies_from_strangers measures real reach.
